question.label: Week-two pulse
question.name: What's been most on your mind at work this week, Riley?
question.description: Gives Riley room to set the agenda before any onboarding friction is named.

answer: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
note: He sounds engaged with the technical material but volunteered a clarity gap about how decisions get made here; worth probing next.

question.label: Decision-making gap
question.name: When you say 'how decisions are really made here,' what feels least clear?
question.description: Drills into his specific uncertainty about team decision-making norms.

answer: I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
note: He can follow current request paths, but missing historical context behind tradeoffs signals a mild remaining clarity gap.

question.label: Tradeoff context
question.name: Which tradeoffs feel hardest to understand the history behind?
question.description: Drills into the specific missing historical context he just named.

answer: I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
note: He names a specific clarity gap: unclear domain ownership when priorities conflict; [THREAD-DEFERRED] ownership boundaries under conflict worth picking up next session.

question.label: Context gaps
question.name: What context are you still missing before you make bigger calls, Riley?
question.description: Finds the places where he may be filling in gaps from docs instead of team norms.

answer: I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
note: He is withholding questions to avoid interrupting people, which is a strong clarity blocker and suggests he lacks a safe path to ask for context.

question.label: Notes doc habit
question.name: That notes doc habit — what makes it hard to ask in the moment?
question.description: Drills into the hesitation to ask so the manager can remove the specific friction.

answer: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
note: Deficiency-as-request: he names a missing escalation guide, which is a strong clarity gap; no direct wellbeing signal.

question.label: Escalate vs batch
question.name: What would a useful escalate-versus-batch guide need to cover?
question.description: Turns the missing escalation guide into concrete onboarding support the manager can provide.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
note: He names a specific onboarding gap: practical incident handoff details are missing from docs and only learned live, which is strong clarity risk and mild wellbeing strain; [THREAD-DEFERRED] practical handoff details during live incidents.

question.label: Team contact
question.name: Who have you had the most useful direct contact with so far, Riley?
question.description: Shows whether his onboarding is still too doc-heavy and where relationships are thin.

answer: A short weekly architecture review with someone senior would help me calibrate faster.
note: Names a specific missing support structure — regular senior architecture review to calibrate faster — which signals ongoing clarity friction and onboarding strain.

question.label: Support needed
question.name: What would be most useful from me over the next couple of weeks, Riley?
question.description: Converts missing context into a concrete manager support plan.

answer: If I know exactly what ownership I should take this month, I can contribute with more confidence.
note: Names missing ownership clarity as the support he lacks right now; that is a mild negative signal on clarity and growth.

question.label: Next-step landing
question.name: Given what we've covered, what should we tighten up before our next check-in?
question.description: Closes on concrete follow-up and keeps the meeting moving toward action.

answer: I will ask one architecture question in the next incident review instead of keeping it in my notes doc.
note: He named a specific next behavior change—asking an architecture question in the incident review—which signals slightly improved clarity and learning orientation.
